title: AberdeenGroup SnapShot: Snap Appliance Inc.
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: This Aberdeen Group SnapShot profiles Snap Appliance Inc., the self-described number-one volume NAS provider, which was reborn as a private company in 2002 after being spun out of Quantum. The profile details Snap's product line spanning workgroup to enterprise NAS using inexpensive ATA disks, and explicitly maps Snap's coverage of Aberdeen's midline storage categories including staging, fixed content, consolidation, replication, and PC backup. The document positions Snap as a key validation of Aberdeen's midline disk storage thesis.
importance_rationale: An important contemporaneous vendor profile validating Aberdeen's midline/ATA storage thesis; one of the earliest published profiles of a dedicated NAS appliance vendor aligned to the emerging tiered storage taxonomy. Kastner's connection to the midline thesis makes this a primary source document.
relevance_rationale: NAS appliance market dynamics and ATA-based midline storage concepts remain relevant; the product architecture (dedicated appliance + inexpensive drives + simple management) anticipated the modern NAS market. However specific product details (Snap Server 1100 etc.) are dated.
prescience_rationale: Correctly identified NAS + ATA as a winning combination for the midline tier — the NAS category thrived enormously. However Snap Appliance itself was acquired by Overland Storage (2008) and absorbed into Sphere 3D (2014); the company did not survive independently despite a strong product thesis.
